3.2. Colloidal stability evaluation by backscattering proﬁles

To evaluate in detail
the colloidal
stability of
the all PEG-ylated co-
loaded nanocarriers the time dependent backscattering proﬁles were mea-

sured using turbidimetric method. The levels of
the backscattering (ex-
pressed in %) are indicated on the ordinate axis, while the height (expressed

in mm) corresponding to diﬀerent
levels of
the colloidal
sample in the
measurement vial is marked on the abscissa axis. By analysing the distances
between the curves in BS proﬁles of the all nanosystems from Table 1 at 0

day (freshly prepared) and after 30 days of
their
storage in room tem-

perature (Fig. 3),
it is possible to determine the dynamics of the processes

occurring in the sample. Generally,
rapid destabilization phenomena are
characterized by a large distance between the curves, while coverage of the
individual curves supports the high stability of the system and slow rate of
destabilization phenomena [5,6]. The ﬁrst graph (Fig. 3a) is an example of

highly unstable system 6 with the evident aggregation process of
the na-
noparticles in the analysed sample, since the BS decrease versus time at the

all

(Table 1), we observe only a slight decrease of the NCs size (from 189 nm to
